question.text: What is the primary role of the financial system in an economy?

answer.text: To transfer funds from surplus units to deficit units efficiently
To regulate the banking sector exclusively
To print and distribute currency notes
To provide insurance coverage to all citizens

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The financial system refers to the entire set of institutionalized arrangements by which funds are transferred from surplus units (households) to deficit units (corporations and governments) at terms acceptable to both sides. **The Trap:** Option B is too narrow — regulation is one function, not the primary role. Options C and D describe functions of the central bank and insurance sector respectively. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Understanding the flow of funds from savers to borrowers is fundamental to grasping how every financial product fits into the broader economy.
